claim: Jimbo M., Miwa T. 'Monodromy preserving deformation of linear ordinary differential equations with rational coefficients. II', Physica D 2 (1981) 407-448, DOI 10.1016/0167-2789(81)90021-X, is the canonical primary source for the 2x2 isomonodromic Lax-pair construction of Painlevé-III(D6).

RIMS preprint number RIMS-327 (Jimbo & Miwa, 1980, 'MONODROMY PRESERVING DEFORMATION OF LINEAR ORDINARY DIFFERENTIAL EQUATIONS WITH RATIONAL COEFFICIENTS. II') is listed at https://www.kurims.kyoto-u.ac.jp/preprint/preprint_y1980.html with bibliographic field '(Physica 2D(1981), 407-448)' confirming match to Phys.D acquisition target.

ScienceDirect direct DOI fetch (https://doi.org/10.1016/0167-2789(81)90021-X) redirects (HTTP 200 chain through linkinghub.elsevier.com) to a paywall landing page with no extractable full-text content for non-institutional users; per Rule 1, no auth flow attempted.

OA acquisition routes B.2 (author institutional pages), B.3 (RIMS preprint archive — listing only, no PDF), B.4 (arXiv — predates 1991), B.5 (ResearchGate HTTP 403 auth-required), B.6 (Internet Archive HTTP 404 / no full-text), and B.7 (HathiTrust — Elsevier copyright) all fail to yield an OA PDF for JM81 II at probe time 2026-05-04.

BLMP 2024 (Barhoumi, Lisovyy, Miller, Prokhorov, SIGMA 20 (2024) 019, arXiv:2307.11217) reference [24] is verbatim 'Jimbo M., Miwa T., Monodromy preserving deformation of linear ordinary differential equations with rational coefficients. II, Phys. D 2 (1981), 407-448.', confirmed by pypdf extraction of slot 08 (SHA-256 96C49CDD51B6C2A395CCD6CC3CB66BFFEB623643B1A2374DB3F203760F696BB3) refs page.

BLMP 2024 §4.1 page 24 attributes the 2x2 isomonodromic Lax-pair construction of Painlevé-III(D6) to JM81 II via the verbatim phrase 'Following Jimbo and Miwa [24], we use the fact that each Painlevé equation can be recast as an isomonodromic deformation condition for a 2x2 system of linear ODEs with rational coefficients.' (slot 08 page 24, pypdf extract).

BLMP 2024 §4.1 equations (4.1)-(4.6) introduce a 2-parameter labeling (ϴ_0, ϴ_∞) for the JM81 II Lax-pair Painlevé-III(D6) construction, with the explicit map to the standard PIII(D6) coefficients (α, β) given by 'ϴ_0 = α/4, ϴ_∞ = 1 - β/4' (verbatim BLMP eq 4.6, page 25).

BLMP 2024 §4.1 formal-monodromy expansions ψ_formal^(∞) ~ exp(ix λ σ_3 / 2) λ^(-ϴ_∞ σ_3 / 2) (eq 4.7) and ψ_formal^(0) ~ exp(-i x λ^(-1) σ_3 / 2) λ^(ϴ_0 σ_3 / 2) (eq 4.8) confirm that ϴ_0 and ϴ_∞ are the (only) formal-monodromy exponents at the two irregular singular points λ=0 and λ=∞ in the JM81 II Lax-pair labeling for PIII(D6).

BLMP 2024 §4.1 transitive reading indicates JM81 II's core Lax-pair convention for Painlevé-III(D6) is 2-parameter (ϴ_0, ϴ_∞), structurally distinct from the 4-tuple (α_∞, α_0, β_∞, β_0) used in CT v1.3 §3.5; the cardinality and indexing differ. Provisional pending direct primary-source verification.

JM81 II's 2-parameter (ϴ_0, ϴ_∞) Lax-pair labeling is structurally identical to the W(B_2) (θ_0, θ_∞) labeling pinned in OKAMOTO-1987-CONSTRAINT-PIN (sessions/2026-05-04/OKAMOTO-1987-CONSTRAINT-PIN/handoff.md verdict UPGRADE_G18_CLOSED_PARAMETER_CORRESPONDENCE_OKAMOTO_2PARAM_VS_CT_4TUPLE), supporting the conclusion that CT v1.3 §3.5's 4-tuple is not derivable from either the JM81 II or Okamoto 1987 core conventions.
